Container.Name: PixooPal
Container.Overview: PixooPal is a server-side clockface engine for your Divoom Pixoo 64.
Container.Description: PixooPal provides a browser UI, live preview, clockfaces, notifications, and direct controls for a Divoom Pixoo 64 display on your local network. Work in progress.

Config.Name: Divoom MITM HTTPS Port
Config.Description: HTTPS port used by app.divoom-gz.com DNS redirection. Pixoo expects this to be reachable as port 443.

Config.Name: Embedded MQTT Broker Port
Config.Description: PixooPal embedded MQTT broker. Pixoo expects MQTT on port 1883.

Config.Name: HTTP_PORT
Config.Description: Container HTTP port PixooPal listens on. Keep this aligned with the WebUI container port unless you know you need to change it.

Config.Name: HTTPS_PORT
Config.Description: Container HTTPS port PixooPal listens on using the bundled app.divoom-gz.com certificate.

Config.Name: Pixoo Device Address
Config.Description: Pixoo host or IP address on your local network.

Config.Name: PixooPal Host IP
Config.Description: PixooPal host IP returned to Pixoo from /Device/InitV2. Must be reachable from the Pixoo device; MQTT always uses port 1883.

Config.Name: Resolution
Config.Description: PixooPal render resolution. Supported values are 16, 32, and 64. Lower values can improve render and push performance.

Config.Name: Home Assistant URL
Config.Description: Home Assistant Address:Port, for example 192.168.1.5:8123. If only an IP is provided, PixooPal uses port 8123.

Config.Name: Home Assistant Token
Config.Description: Long-lived Home Assistant access token. Generate one from Security tab in your profile.

Config.Name: Debug Logging
Config.Description: Set to true to include additional queue, clockface, and Pixoo command diagnostics in logs.

Config.Name: Disable mDNS Discovery
Config.Description: Set to true to disable PixooPal mDNS/Bonjour discovery announcements.
